summary: Added a stale-live-proof diagnostic for Codex Desktop pre-response verification, proved older Codex Desktop hook evidence still passes, bumped the app to v2.2.3, pushed the app commit/tag, and regenerated the Windows installer ZIP/SHA. The overall closed-loop goal remains partial because verify:goal still requires a fresh real Codex Desktop hook proof after the latest hook/server update.
decisions: Do not weaken the live pre-response proof freshness gate; distinguish stale valid evidence from missing hook evidence instead.
Treat the existing 2026-07-07T17:47Z Codex Desktop hook proof as real but stale because the latest build/server timestamp is newer.
Use v2.2.3 rather than moving v2.2.2 because the v2.2.2 tag already exists on the remote.
next_steps: Run npm run codex:live-proof and paste the proof prompt into a fresh Codex Desktop workspace thread after the latest build.
Rerun npm run verify:goal; the expected remaining blocker should clear only after a new codex_desktop hook report is written.
If GitHub Release asset upload is required, provide or configure a GitHub token and run scripts/publish-github-release.ps1 without -SkipUpload.
